headline: Figure AI (ai-labs entry) first agent baseline. Dual-presence entity: separate ai-labs entry (composite 37.5, developing band) and robotics-labs entry (composite 48.4, functional band — assessed separately today). The ai-labs entry captures Figure AI as AI-model entity (Helix family of models, post-OpenAI partnership, Microsoft Azure-deployed). Compound 2025-2026 evidence: (1) March 2024 OpenAI partnership terminated 2024 — Figure pivoted to in-house Helix model development; (2) Helix model architecture (vision-language-action transformer for humanoid control) deployed in BMW Spartanburg pilot; (3) no published Helix model card or system card; (4) ASTM safety-standards working group participation (cross-listed with robotics-labs entry); (5) aggressive 2026 commercial scope including consumer-home-robot ambitions; (6) Figure AI is NOT a 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory. Published 37.5 / Developing band sustained — AI-model accountability gap (no model/system card) + OpenAI-partnership-termination governance instability + aggressive deployment timeline aggregate to developing-band placement. Sub-threshold downward pressure (~-2.0) considered for ACC (no model card) and INT (governance instability post-OpenAI). Recommend confirm at 37.5 with first-baseline rationale.
key_evidence: March 2024 OpenAI partnership: Figure AI announced strategic OpenAI partnership for advanced language models in humanoid control; partnership terminated by 2024.
Helix model architecture: Figure's in-house vision-language-action transformer for humanoid control, replacing OpenAI dependency; deployed in BMW Spartanburg pilot.
No published Helix model card, system card, or AI-safety policy specific to humanoid-control AI deployment.
Microsoft Azure infrastructure deployment for Helix training and inference.
ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation (cross-listed with Figure AI robotics-labs entry).
Aggressive 2026 commercial scope: BMW production-line + Figure 03 home-robot ambitions in same year (CEO Adcock framing).
Figure AI is NOT a 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory.
$675M Series B at $2.6B valuation context (February 2024); investor base includes Microsoft, OpenAI Startup Fund, Nvidia, Jeff Bezos.

evidence.source: vfuturemedia — Humanoid robots enter the workforce: Figure, Boston Dynamics, Tesla Optimus 2026
evidence.finding: Figure 03 BMW Spartanburg pilot ongoing; aggressive 2026 commercial timeline; Helix model deployment.

evidence.source: Interesting Engineering — Science fiction become reality in 2026
The Robot Report — Agility, Boston Dynamics, ASTM to discuss state of humanoid robotics
evidence.finding: Figure AI participation in ASTM safety-standards working group with Agility and Boston Dynamics.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 confirmed. A1 detection: BMW pilot context provides operational-issue surfacing; A4 amplification: ASTM working-group participation amplifies safety signals.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: Helix model design framed around humanoid-task completion rather than empathic-regard architecture explicitly.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: BMW pilot operational; AC4 response: incident-response framework not publicly documented.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: aggressive consumer-deployment ambition without published equity-impact analysis; AI-model deployment concentrates capability among well-resourced industrial partners.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: NOT an anti-weaponization pledge signatory; no published Helix scope-restriction policy. B5 consent: AI-model consent framework not publicly documented.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 confirmed. AB2 transparency: no Helix model card, no system card, no AI-safety policy publication. AB4 reparation undefined for AI-model harm scenarios.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: ASTM working-group participation indicates systemic-harm reasoning at industry level; aggressive deployment pace is SYS-erosive sub-threshold.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 confirmed. I1 consistency: declared safety commitments + ASTM participation; OpenAI-partnership-termination indicates governance instability (sub-threshold INT-erosive); absence of anti-weaponization pledge is INT-erosive sub-threshold.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 37.5. Developing band sustained. Confirm with first-baseline rationale; sub-threshold downward pressure documented (ACC, INT) but does not yield material change.

negativeSignals: No published Helix model card, system card, or AI-safety policy; OpenAI-partnership-termination 2024 indicates governance instability; not an anti-weaponization pledge signatory; aggressive 2026 commercial scope (BMW + home-robot ambitions); Microsoft Azure infrastructure proximity to Pentagon classified cohort (Microsoft is cohort member).
positiveSignals: ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation (cross-listed with robotics-labs entry); BMW Spartanburg commercial pilot operational; Helix in-house model development indicates reduced OpenAI dependency; high-profile investor base (Microsoft, OpenAI Startup Fund, Nvidia, Jeff Bezos) provides governance accountability potential.
sector_context: AI Labs / humanoid-control-AI cluster. Cross-reference: Figure AI robotics-labs entry (assessed separately today, composite 48.4), OpenAI (HOLD until May 21), Microsoft (HOLD until ~May 15-20), Pentagon classified cohort (cross-cutting). Figure AI ai-labs entry distinguished by Helix in-house model + BMW pilot + OpenAI-partnership-termination + dual-presence (robotics-labs + ai-labs).
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with developing-band confirmation
Cross-reference to Figure AI robotics-labs entry registered
open_actions_carried: Watch: any Helix model card, system card, or AI-safety policy publication
Watch: BMW deployment scaling and any AI-model harm or incident report
Watch: any anti-weaponization pledge signing event
Watch: ASTM May 27-28 2026 Boston convening outcomes
Watch: dual-entry alignment (robotics-labs entry composite 48.4 vs ai-labs entry composite 37.5 — methodologically distinct entities)
